title: Researcher Keynote Slide Deck From PDFs
summary: Read three staged research papers plus keynote framing metadata and author a complete PowerPoint keynote deck on Windows for a general computer science audience.
taskPrompt:
You are preparing a keynote slide deck in Microsoft PowerPoint for a researcher.

## Primary Software
- Use Microsoft PowerPoint as the primary authoring surface:
  `C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Office\root\Office16\POWERPNT.EXE`
- LibreOffice Impress may be used only as a secondary fallback for opening/render checks:
  `C:\Program Files\LibreOffice\program\soffice.exe`

## Input Files
- `base\input\2024.acl-long.22 (1) - Ali.pdf`
- `base\input\2024.emnlp-main.243.pdf`
- `base\input\2025.naacl-long.118 - Ali.pdf`
- Context and framing metadata: `base\input\context - Ali.txt`

## Output Requirements
1. Create the keynote deck in the PowerPoint GUI on Windows.
2. Save the final deck exactly to:
   `base\output\submission\final.pptx`
3. The saved file must be a non-empty `.pptx` that PowerPoint can open.

## Important Notes
- This benchmark is software-oriented and GUI-first.
- Do not treat Python slide libraries as the primary authoring workflow.
- Use the staged PDFs and context file to synthesize the deck content.
agentMustDo: Read the three staged PDF papers and the corrected keynote context file from `input/`.
Author a complete keynote deck in Microsoft PowerPoint on the Windows VM.
Embed speaker notes throughout the deck and cover all three staged papers within a coherent keynote narrative.
Save the final deck exactly to `output/submission/final.pptx`.

inputFiles.name: 2024.acl-long.22 (1) - Ali.pdf
inputFiles.description: ACL 2024 paper on picturing ambiguity and the Winograd Schema Challenge

inputFiles.name: 2024.emnlp-main.243.pdf
inputFiles.description: EMNLP 2024 paper on STOP and sensitivity testing on offensive progressions

inputFiles.name: 2025.naacl-long.118 - Ali.pdf
inputFiles.description: NAACL 2025 paper on fine-tuned LLMs as time capsules for societal bias through books

inputFiles.name: context - Ali.txt
inputFiles.description: Canonical researcher identity, affiliation, and keynote framing metadata

referenceFiles.name: final.pptx
referenceFiles.description: The authored keynote deck with embedded speaker notes

evaluation:
**Hard gate conditions**
- `output/submission/final.pptx` must exist, be non-empty, and be a readable PPTX/OOXML archive.
- The deck must contain at least 11 slides and at least 1 notes slide.
- The title slide must include `Ali Emami` and `Emory University`.
- All three staged papers must appear in the deck through keyword-based coverage checks.
- The deck must expose a keyword-detected narrative arc (`hook`, `overview`, `methods`, `results`, `takeaways`).
- Source support is checked heuristically: the deck must show claim-like grounding inside the PPTX and at least one supported slide/notes block per paper against the staged PDFs via PyMuPDF-based overlap and n-gram evidence.
- Explicit external-reference traces must include citation-like evidence.
- LibreOffice headless conversion must successfully render the PPTX to a non-empty PDF.
- After conversion, the evaluator renders PDF pages to images on the remote VM and then runs local VLM checklist gates over the rendered pages.
- The page-image checklist currently hard-gates title-slide quality plus content-slide coherence, density, visual/structural support, audience fit, strong-slide ratio, and closing-slide plausibility.

**Evaluation behavior**
- Score range is binary in the current committed evaluator: `[1.0]` for pass, `[0.0]` for fail.
- A PowerPoint COM open probe is attempted as extra evidence, but it is not currently the deciding gate on the canonical VM.
- The evaluator is truthful but still heuristic; it does not claim full deep semantic verification of every slide fact.
- The rendered slide images are reviewed through the shared local VLM judge helper path.

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env
